Address
950 FLOWER STREET, INGLEWOOD, CA 90301

Setting
Urban
5 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 5/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.04 4.07 3.83 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.31 0.71 0.30 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.35 0.90 1.28 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.37 2.46 2.25 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.66 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.11 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.70
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.24
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.51
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
26.3%
annual
RN turnover
42.9%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.44
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.05
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-11-21 13 13 0 64 1 64
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-10-13 14 11 3 56 1 56
Total weighted health score
62.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (33)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

33 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0847 Inform resident or representatives choice to enter into binding arbitration agreement and right to refuse. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-10
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0790 Provide routine and 24-hour emergency dental care for each resident. D 2024-10-13 2024-10-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-08-06 2024-09-05
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-04-24 2024-04-25
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2023-10-13 2023-10-31
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-10-13 2023-10-31
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-10-13 2023-10-31
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-10-13 2023-10-31
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2023-10-13 2023-10-31
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-10-13 2023-10-31
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-04-21 2023-04-28

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
